anomalies.description: Sudden 90% drop in death benefit value
anomalies.recommendation: Investigate policy adjustment or potential data entry error
Review rider charges and policy amendments
Verify payment method and contact policyholder

Description of Terms
Annual Maintenance Fee: The Annual Maintenance Fee is a flat fee that may be deducted from your Contract Value to reimburse us for 
expenses relating to the administrative maintenance of the contract and your account. The annual charge may be deducted on a Contract 
Anniversary or when the Contract is fully Surrendered and is deducted proportionately from each account in which you are invested.
Contingent Deferred Sales Charge (CDSC): The deferred sales charge, if applicable, may apply when you make a full Surrender or 
Partial Withdrawal.
Optional Benefit(s) Fee: If applicable, Optional Benefit(s) fees may be assessed quarterly to your contract. Please review the Optional 
Benefit(s) section detailed in this statement, along with your prospectus and contract.
Premium Based Charge: We may apply a quarterly Premium Based Charge, if applicable, against all Premium Payments based on 
Remaining Gross Premiums. The Premium Based Charge may be assessed only with respect to Contract Value invested in Sub-Accounts 
and not investments in the Fixed Account.
Remaining Gross Premium: Equals the Premium Payments adjusted by Partial Withdrawals. During the CDSC period, Premium 
Payments will be adjusted for Partial Withdrawals in excess of the free withdrawal amount; after the CDSC period, Premium Payments will 
be adjusted for all Partial Withdrawals. 
Annuity Commencement Date: The first day of the first period for which a distribution is received as an Annuity Payout under the 
Contract.
Additional Information
E-Delivery – Easy for you. Easy on the environment. 
Electronic delivery is a fast, convenient, and environmentally friendly way to receive statements, prospectuses, and other
important account documents from us.  Eliminate mailed paper copies and choose eDelivery today.  Visit 
www.globalatlantic.com
If you find a discrepancy in this statement, please promptly notify us at the address or telephone number provided 
on the statement. Any oral communications from us or your brokerage firm should be re-confirmed in writing to 
protect your rights.
Information concerning compensation arrangements may be found in the prospectus.  For further compensation information, 
please contact the broker/dealer listed in the “Contact Information” section.
Forethought Life Insurance Company is a subsidiary of Global Atlantic Financial Group Limited.
Please note that there are other fees you pay under your variable annuity that, although not individually listed here, could be 
significant. They are detailed in your prospectus. In general, those fees consist of ongoing charges assessed against the 
separate account that supports your annuity and ongoing charges assessed against the assets of any fund to which you may 
have allocated contract value.  All such charges are reflected in values shown in this statement.  Please consult your 
prospectus and contract for details. You may obtain a prospectus by calling us at 866-645-2449.
For information regarding fund performance and service forms, please visit  www.globalatlantic.com
All withdrawals of taxable amounts, including earnings, are taxable as ordinary income and, if taken prior to age 59 ½, may be 
subject to a 10% federal tax penalty.  Withdrawals may reduce the death benefit and the Surrender value.
Continuous or periodic investment plans neither ensure a profit nor protect against a loss in declining markets.  Because these 
programs involve continuous investing regardless of fluctuating price levels, you should carefully consider your financial ability 
to continue through periods of fluctuating market price.
Investing in a variable annuity through a tax-advantaged retirement plan such as an IRA provides no additional tax advantage 
from the variable annuity and should only be considered if it makes sense because of the annuity’s other features, such as 
lifetime income payments or death benefit protection.
Nothing contained in this correspondence is intended to be, and should not be, construed as investment advice.
